methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $3.5B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Apex Space

private.name: Stoke Space
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series D), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Gilmour Space Technologies, an Australian Space & Aerospace company at Series D stage having raised approximately A$100M, is evaluated against a peer universe of 3 same-stage private comparables (Astranis, Apex Space, and Stoke Space) and 5 public-market anchors in the space sector (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

The peer-median method applies directly. The three Series D comparables span a $1.0B–$3.5B range, with a median of $1.0B. This method is the primary quantitative constraint available; the public-comp anchoring approach cannot be executed because the target's revenue is not disclosed, precluding application of the sector's 64.2x P/S median. The peer median therefore implies a valuation of $1.0B.

Analysis supports a narrow range of $850M–$1.1B, centered on $1.0B. This reflects reliance on a single applicable method with limited comparables (n=3) and a wide interquartile spread ($1.0B–$3.5B). Key uncertainties include the applicability of the Astranis comp at the high end—$3.5B—which may reflect unique attributes not present in Gilmour's profile. Revenue and path to positive unit economics remain unspecified and represent material valuation drivers in this early-revenue or pre-revenue sector. Refinement would require disclosure of top-line metrics and capital efficiency indicators relative to peers.
